sections.heading: Benefits of the Versioned Atlas Administration API
sections.body:
The new versioned Atlas Administration API gives a significantly better
overall experience in accessing Atlas programmatically. The
versioned Atlas Administration API provides the following:

sections.heading: Clear Separation of Breaking and Non-Breaking Changes
sections.body:
Atlas introduces all new features with breaking changes in a new
resource version. The current, stable resource version still includes
features that contain non-breaking changes. When Atlas introduces
a new resource version, you can continue to use the current stable resource
version until it is fully deprecated while you develop and test on the
new API (Application Programming Interface) resource version.

sections.heading: Extended Support and Deprecation Periods
sections.body:
Atlas deprecates and then removes resource versions only when it
introduces a new stable API (Application Programming Interface) resource version. Atlas continues
to support the older resource version for use in your production
environment for up to twelve months, which provides you with time to
plan and execute your migration to the next version.

sections.heading: Versioning Schema
sections.body:
Starting with the new Versioned Atlas Administration API (v2),
Atlas Administration API version is based on the lifecycle of the
Atlas Administration API resource.

sections.heading: Versioned Atlas Administration API Lifecycle
sections.body:
Starting in Versioned Atlas Administration API (v2), Atlas Administration API
endpoints go through the `preview`, `stable`, `deprecated`, and `sunset`
stages in the lifecycle.

> **Important:** The stages only apply to Atlas Administration API endpoints in Versioned
> Atlas Administration API (`v2`) of the resource. These stages don't
> affect Atlas Administration API endpoints in resource `v1.0` or
> `v1.5`. Atlas exposes all legacy (v1.0 and v1.5)
> Atlas Administration API resources in its first release on 2023-02-01 in
> `stable` state.

sections.heading: Illustrated API Resource Lifecycle
sections.body:
The following example illustrates the lifecycle of a resource and its
impact on versioning.

![Illustration of admin API versioning lifecycle for a specific API resource.](/api/images/admin-api-versioning-strategy-2.png)

The preceding illustration shows the following:

| Release Date | Release Details                                                                                            | API Lifecycle                                                                                                                                                                             |
| ------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 2024-01-01   | A new stable version is released.                                                                          | #stable: application/vnd.atlas.2024-01-01+json                                                                                                                                            |
| 2025-01-01   | A new stable version is released.The 2024-01-01 version is deprecated.                                     | #deprecated: application/vnd.atlas.2024-01-01+json#stable: application/vnd.atlas.2025-01-01+json                                                                                          |
| 2026-01-01   | The 2024-01-01 version is sunset.A new preview version is introduced, and then deprecated within the year. | #sunset: application/vnd.atlas.2024-01-01+json#stable: application/vnd.atlas.2025-01-01+json#preview: application/vnd.atlas.preview+json                                                  |
| 2027-01-01   | A new stable version is released.The 2025-01-01 version is deprecated.The preview version is sunset.       | #sunset: application/vnd.atlas.2024-01-01+json#deprecated: application/vnd.atlas.2025-01-01+json#sunset: application/vnd.atlas.preview+json#stable: application/vnd.atlas.2027-01-01+json |
| 2028-01-01   | A new stable version is released.The 2025-01-01 version is sunset.                                         | #sunset:application/vnd.atlas.2024-01-01+jsonapplication/vnd.atlas.2025-01-01+jsonapplication/vnd.atlas.preview+json#stable: application/vnd.atlas.2027-01-01+json                        |

sections.heading: API (Application Programming Interface) Endpoints
sections.body:
For legacy unversioned Atlas Administration API endpoints, Atlas
introduced new and breaking changes in the same version. Starting in
versioned Atlas Administration API (v2), Atlas introduces breaking
changes only in a new resource version. Atlas continues to
introduce non-breaking changes to existing `stable` version. Use the
release date of a resource to submit a request to a specific version of
a versioned Atlas Administration API (v2) endpoint.

The following table shows the legacy API (Application Programming Interface) endpoint versions, whether
the version has a corresponding versioned Atlas Administration API (v2)
endpoint, and the release date of the versioned Atlas Administration API
(v2) resource. Note that an equivalent of v1.0 in the unversioned
Atlas Administration API is represented by `2023-01-01` in the versioned
Atlas Administration API, which is now deprecated.

| Version Number | Supported in v2? | v2 Resource Version (in yyyy-mm-dd format) |
| -------------- | ---------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| v1.0           | √                | 2023-01-01                                 |
| v1.5           | √                | 2023-02-01                                 |

sections.heading: Next Steps
sections.body:
- Review the [API changelog](versioned-api-changelog), which
  gives you visibility into all API (Application Programming Interface) changes across different versions.

- Review the [API Specification](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec), which contains information for all
  stable and deprecated resource versions.

- Follow the steps in the [Migration Guide](atlas-migrate-api-version) to migrate from the current unversioned
  Atlas Administration API to the new versioned Atlas Administration API.
